normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem precond_nonempty {a : Array Int} (h : a.size > 0) : True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice/array is non-empty (size > 0), matching the task requirement that the slice is non-empty.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def maxContigSum (xs : List Int) : Int := by
  -- maximum over all contiguous non-empty sublists
  -- (formalized using a fold over all start/end indices or all sublists with proofs of contiguity)
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the mathematical specification: the maximum sum among all contiguous non-empty sublists of the input list.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def rust_impl_result (xs : List Int) : Int := by
  -- model the Rust reference implementation as a pure function on lists
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define a pure Lean function that corresponds to the Rust reference implementation’s computed result.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem rust_impl_total (xs : List Int) : ∃ r : Int, rust_impl_result xs = r
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The reference implementation is total on non-empty inputs (i.e., it produces an integer result without getting stuck).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem rust_impl_correct_spec {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) : rust_impl_result xs = maxContigSum xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The value computed by the Rust implementation equals the specification: the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSum_is_upper_bound {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∀ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length →
    let sub := xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) in
    sum sub ≤ maxContigSum xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For every contiguous non-empty subarray (defined by indices i<j), its sum is ≤ the computed maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSum_attained {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∃ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧
    let sub := xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) in
    sum sub = maxContigSum xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists at least one contiguous non-empty subarray whose sum equals the maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSum_nonempty {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∃ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Because the input is non-empty, there exists at least one contiguous non-empty subarray to take a maximum over.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem sum_drop_take_matches_subarray {xs : List Int} (i j : Nat)
  (hi : i < j) (hj : j ≤ xs.length) :
  sum (xs.drop i |>.take (j - i)) = sum (subarray xs i j)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Ensure the Lean definition of the subarray sum used in the specification matches the intended contiguous subarray notion (drop/take vs an explicit subarray function).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem kadane_invariant {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  -- If the implementation is modeled as Kadane’s algorithm,
  -- prove the key invariant relating the running variables to
  -- best prefix/suffix sums over processed elements.
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the core loop invariant of the algorithm: the maintained running values correspond to the best (maximum) sums of contiguous subarrays ending at the current position (and/or the best overall so far).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem rust_impl_final_uses_invariant {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  -- Show that after processing all elements, the maintained 'best overall'
  -- equals the maximum over all contiguous non-empty subarrays.
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Use the invariant to conclude that the final returned value equals the global maximum over all contiguous non-empty subarrays.
